commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: version comparison extracts semver from openclaw --version output

openclaw --version returns "OpenClaw 2026.3.8 (3caab92)" while npm
dist-tags returns "2026.3.8". Direct string comparison always showed
update available even when versions matched. Now extracts semver for
comparison and uses backend updateAvailable flag in frontend.

commit.message:
fix(api): Return status 'up' with degraded flag for non-fatal health checks

Terminus v11 HealthCheckExecutor treats resolved values with
status 'down' as errors, causing 503 even when the promise doesn't
reject. Change storage/telegram catch handlers to return status 'up'
with a degraded flag so the overall /health/detailed endpoint stays
200 when only optional services are unavailable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use OpenAI native JSON mode — eliminates parse failures and retry waste

callGPTJSON now uses response_format: { type: "json_object" } instead of
text-based "respond only with JSON" instruction. The model is forced to
output valid JSON — no more markdown wrapping, no truncated JSON from
format confusion, no wasted retry budget on parse failures.

Before: text instruction → JSON.parse fails → retry with correction prompt → timeout
After: native JSON mode → always valid JSON → only retry on rare schema mismatch

commit.message:
fix(web): preserve /api prefix in Next.js rewrite destination

The rewrite was stripping the /api prefix, causing /api/v1/health
to proxy to ${API_URL}/v1/health instead of ${API_URL}/api/v1/health.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Batch of UI/UX polish improvements across frontend and backend

Add a formatDate utility (utils.ts) that renders ISO date strings as
human-readable long-form dates (e.g. "Sunday, March 8, 2026"), and apply
it to the edition cover component and the editions admin listing.

Allow pressing Enter to save when editing a source name inline, avoiding
the need to click the save button.

Support editing article body text by adding extracted_text to the
ArtifactUpdate schema on the backend and exposing a textarea on the
artifact edit page. This lets admins correct or amend extracted content
without re-running the worker.

Improve article typography by allowing <hr> through the DOMPurify
sanitizer and adding CSS drop-cap styling on the first paragraph and on
paragraphs that follow a section break, giving the magazine layout more
visual character.

Introduce an /about help page explaining how to use the app (saving
links, editions, notes) and add a HelpCircle icon link in the nav bar
so all users can find it.

Add a subtle bottom border to the sticky nav header to visually separate
it from page content below.

Implement full author CRUD: backend endpoints in admin.py (list, create,
update, delete with artifact-count aggregation), frontend admin page at
/admin/authors, API client functions, and author association checkboxes
on the artifact edit page so articles can be tagged with their authors.

Update README to reflect the new authors entity, about page, and utils
module in the project structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rebalance scoring thresholds; use Azure PronScore directly

Color levels:
- Green: acc >= 75 and ErrorType = None
- Yellow: acc 50-74, or Mispronunciation (acc >= 50)
- Red: acc < 50, or Omission

Total score: use Azure PronScore as-is (drop the 70/30 blend)

commit.message:
Initial implementation of Wave accounting MCP server

6 FastMCP tools wrapping the full Wave GraphQL API (52 operations):
- account: chart of accounts CRUD + archive + search
- party: customers (full CRUD) + vendors (read-only)
- transaction: invoices (full lifecycle + payments) + money transactions (BETA)
- item: products & services CRUD + archive + search
- reference: businesses, users, currencies, countries, sales taxes, estimates
- report: synthesized business summary, account balances, customer balances

Static Bearer token auth, async httpx with rate-limit backoff,
dual transport (STDIO/SSE on port 3076).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: wave-mcp

commit.message:
Add search_symbols and search_text MCP tools with FTS5 sanitization

Implement full-text search tools powered by SQLite FTS5 virtual tables.
search_symbols searches the symbol index by name/signature/doc comments.
search_text searches raw file contents with optional glob filtering.
Add Fts5QuerySanitizer with allow-list approach that strips column
filters, NEAR, caret operators, and balances quotes/parentheses.
Malformed queries gracefully fall back to literal phrase search.

commit.message:
fix(deploy): remove output:standalone from web/site Next.js configs

Railway RAILPACK detects output:"standalone" and runs the standalone
server (node .next/standalone/server.js), which does NOT serve
.next/static/ files — causing all JS chunks to 404. Removing
standalone makes RAILPACK use `next start` which serves everything.

The Dockerfiles can still be used with standalone by adding the
output config back for Docker-only builds if needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

sec: SEC-003 Proteger Arena Operations contra race conditions

Adicionada proteção com sync.Mutex em operações de arena nas camadas:

- GlobalAttention: Lock/Unlock em ForwardWithArena ao salvar input
- Embedding: Lock/Unlock em ForwardWithArena ao salvar índices
- LayerNorm: Lock/Unlock em ForwardWithArena ao salvar input/means/stds
- RMSNorm: Lock/Unlock em ForwardWithArena ao salvar input/RMS
- SwiGLU: Lock/Unlock nos dois blocos de arena (input e pre-activations)
- Bidirectional: Lock/Unlock nos dois blocos (input e forward output)

Já existia proteção em:
- Dense (layer.go)
- Conv2D, Conv1D
- BatchNorm2D
- LSTM, GRU
- AvgPool2D, Dropout

Esta proteção previne corrupção de memória quando múltiplas
goroutines acedem à arena partilhada em workloads paralelos.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Release v4.4.3: Codebase audit — regex escape, atomic writes, runtime validators, constants extraction

Implemented comprehensive codebase audit with fixes across 7 severity tiers:

Tier 1 - Critical Bugs:
- Fix file trigger regex escape missing * and ? characters (regex injection)
- Fix background task store race condition with atomic writes
- Improve embedder preload failure visibility (DEBUG → WARN)
- Relax timing-dependent performance test

Tier 2 - Type Safety:
- Add asCategory() runtime validator for safe DB row category conversion
- Replace 6 unsafe as MemoryCategory casts in recall.ts and prune.ts
- Document AdminArgs → MemoryArgs cast safety rationale
- Document mutateAndSave() opt-out contract

Tier 3 - Robustness:
- Extract magic numbers to named constants: RELEVANCE_EPSILON, ACCESS_SCORE_SATURATION_DEFAULT
- Add tag validation to reject empty strings
- Use extracted constants in recall.ts and scoring.ts

Tier 4 - Architecture:
- Add composite database index idx_memories_project_scope for (project, scope) queries

Tier 5 - Tests:
- Add admin-handler.test.ts with routing coverage
- Relax performance test timing bounds for CI

Tier 6 - Documentation:
- Fix CLAUDE.md constants reference (ONE_DAY_MS → MS_PER_DAY)
- Add asCategory() and handleGoal() mutation gotchas
- Clarify saveEntityStore() mitigation and CRUD examples

All 2180 tests pass. TypeScript compiles cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add DB cache for album panel to survive Spotify rate limits

Previously every album panel open required a live Spotify API call.
Now tracks and artist data are stored in the albums table on first fetch
(TTL 7 days) so subsequent opens are served entirely from SQLite.

- db/index.js: migrations for tracks_json, artists_json, detail_cached_at
- routes/albums.js: check DB cache first; only call Spotify on cache miss;
  upsert cache after successful Spotify fetch; include _cached flag in response

refactor: fix architecture violations and add workflow gate

Fix architecture violations where Server Actions bypassed the domain
layer with direct Drizzle queries. All actions now wire through use
cases via createServerContainer(). Also add workflow gate to CLAUDE.md
to prevent recurrence.

Changes:
- Add ## Workflow section to CLAUDE.md with /create-issue step
- Add import 'server-only' guards to lib/db.ts, lib/auth.ts, lib/schema.ts
- Fix ServerContainer interface to use domain abstractions (no *Impl types)
- Wire all Server Actions through use cases (transactions, categories, budget-settings, dashboard)
- Create missing DeleteBudgetSettingUseCase
- Move actions from src/app/actions/ to src/presentation/features/*/actions/
- Fix schema imports → domain entity imports in presentation layer
- Extract ViewModel hooks for TransactionDetailView, TransactionNewView, BudgetSettingNewView, BudgetSettingEditView, SetupView
- Replace setFilters raw setter with applyFilters named wrapper in useTransactionsViewModel
- Import DashboardData type from domain in useDashboardViewModel
- Accept redirectOrigin constructor param in AuthDataSourceImpl (SSR-safe)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(api): Fix audit_logs NULL violation and remove duplicate interceptor registration

AuditSubscriber.createAuditLog() never set metadata or tags fields,
causing NULL value insertion into audit_logs where the DB enforces
NOT NULL. This broke audit logging on every login. Fix: always
provide metadata={} and tags=[] defaults.

CommonModule registered LoggingInterceptor, TransformInterceptor,
TimeoutInterceptor, HttpExceptionFilter, and ValidationPipe as global
APP_* providers — but AppModule already registers the same ones.
NestJS instantiated both, causing every request to be logged twice,
every response transformed twice, etc. Remove the duplicate
registrations from CommonModule, keep only AppLoggerService.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix EV charging too fast when battery discharging (v1.0.34)

When the home battery is discharging to support EV charging, the grid
import (p1) appears artificially low, making PM think solar covers the
EV load. This caused EV to charge at 14A (9.7kW) with only 4.3kW PV,
rapidly draining the battery.

Fix: subtract battery discharge power from available_power so EV amps
match actual solar production (e.g., 6A for 4.3kW PV instead of 14A).

fix: test generation stability — dynamic tokens, smaller hard chunks, partial results

- MAX_RETRIES 1→2, TIME_BUDGET 40s→50s for more retry room
- CHUNK_SIZE 10→7 for hard difficulty (verbose output)
- Dynamic maxTokens per chunk (difficulty×questions+overhead vs flat 4000)
- Detect finish_reason=length in GPT calls (truncated JSON → descriptive error)
- Per-chunk error isolation: one failed chunk doesn't kill parallel batch
- Accept partial results instead of failing (7/10 questions > 0)
- JSON retry budget 15s→20s in callGPTJSON
